signal_title: Justice Department Settlement on Social Media Coercion
risk_rationale: The DOJ settlement introduces a requirement to prevent government-induced content suppression, which may conflict with TikTok's current content moderation practices that involve cooperation with government requests. This poses a material compliance gap that requires immediate attention to align with the new legal expectations.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Enforcement framework includes documented public-interest exception categories with safety caveats.
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Implementation of measures to prevent government-induced content suppression.
compliance_gaps.gap_description: Current policy allows for content moderation based on public-interest exceptions, which may include government requests. The new regulation requires measures to prevent suppression induced by government influence.

remediations.title: Implement Government Influence Mitigation Measures
remediations.description: Develop and deploy a system to track and review government requests for content moderation to ensure compliance with free speech protections. Establish clear guidelines and training for moderation teams to identify and resist undue government influence.
acceptance_criteria: System for tracking government requests is operational
Guidelines for identifying undue influence are documented and distributed
Training sessions for moderation teams are completed

business_impact: Failure to comply with the DOJ settlement could result in legal challenges and reputational damage, potentially affecting user trust and platform engagement.
recommended_actions: Review and update content moderation policies to align with free speech protections
Engage with legal counsel to ensure compliance with the DOJ settlement
Communicate policy changes to moderation teams and provide necessary training
